title: Classes, Modifiers, Private State, and Abstract Contracts · Contract Lens: Parameter Properties
summary: Implement scheduling components with runtime invariants, replaceable policies, and explicit construction rules. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts parameter properties increment in a typed scheduling component library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.
Use public, protected, TypeScript private, and JavaScript hash-private fields with their actual compile-time and runtime boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
